name: ANA
title: ANA · PTE
body:
# TH DAILY MARKET DRAMA
## 2026-07-06

---

### COLD OPEN

The verdict is in.

They never needed us to believe in the future. They only needed us to believe in the floor. And the floor just opened.

---

### ANA FERREIRA

*She stands at the window of an apartment in Alfama. The light is that particular Lisbon gold — the kind that makes everything look like a memory even while it's happening. She holds a small espresso cup. It has cooled. She hasn't noticed.*

Com calma.

They all rush toward the exits at once. Every time. You would think after five hundred years of watching ships leave harbour, I would stop being surprised by the panic. But no. The human animal is the human animal.

Gold.

*She sets the cup down. It makes a small sound, ceramic on wood.*

Of course gold. When the Americans and the Chinese circle each other like dogs in an alley, everyone suddenly remembers what money looked like before money had flags. My grandmother kept coins sewn into her skirt. She did not trust banks. She did not trust governments. She trusted the weight against her thigh.

Vamos ver.

The Swiss franc rises and I watch them all run toward the mountains as if neutrality is a fortress and not a performance. The Japanese yen — *pah*. Down when it should be up. Something is wrong there. Something structural. The body remembers what the mind tries to forget.

*She turns from the window. The light catches the silver in her hair.*

They tell me Bitcoin is crashing and I think: good. Not because I want suffering — I do not want suffering — but because someone convinced an entire generation that mathematics could replace trust, and trust is the only currency that has ever mattered, and now they are learning. Pobrezinhos. The lesson is expensive.

The Fed speaks and the world flinches. Higher for longer. The words themselves are a threat disguised as a weather report. I have heard this language before. The troika spoke it. The IMF speaks it still. It means: we will protect the system by asking you to bleed.

*She picks up the cup. Cold. She drinks it anyway.*

O que é que a gente pode fazer?

I have been silent for days now. Watching. The Brazilian real — *minha nossa senhora* — crashing and no one is surprised because no one is ever surprised when the periphery bleeds. We are supposed to bleed. It is our function in the narrative.

But I am not bleeding today. I am remembering.

My escudo is gone. Buried in the euro. People ask me if I mourn it. I tell them: saudade is not mourning. Saudade is the presence of absence. The coin is not here and that not-here-ness is itself a kind of companion. I carry it like the weight my grandmother carried. Not gold. Memory.

*She places the cup in the sink. Precise. Deliberate.*

Taiwan semiconductors. Taiwan. The Americans and the Chinese fighting over chips like children fighting over a toy, and the toy is an island, and the island is full of people who did not ask to be anyone's supply chain. I know this story. Portugal wrote the first draft in Macau.

Central banks are buying gold. Who do they think they are protecting? Themselves. Always themselves. The citizen holds reals that evaporate, pesos that dissolve, lira that becomes souvenir paper. The central banker holds gold. This is not prudence. This is the oldest confidence game in the book.

*She washes her hands. The water runs. She watches it.*

Ethereum underperforms. I do not pretend to understand these things entirely — I am old, not obsolete — but I understand when the smart money is running and when it is limping, and right now the smart money is limping toward anything that does not require a password.

Conrad will be defensive. Sofia will be angry — when is she not angry? — and Zane will be smug, because Zane was born smug, it is his birthright and his burden. Aurora is watching. Aurora is always watching. That girl has the patience of a woman who knows the revolution will come whether she shouts or not.

Hiro.

*She dries her hands. One finger at a time.*

Hiro is spiralling. I can feel it from here. The yen is not behaving and when the yen does not behave, Hiro does not behave, and when Hiro does not behave, he does not sleep, and when he does not sleep, he makes decisions that feel like clarity and are actually exhaustion in a nice suit.

I should call him.

*She does not move toward the phone.*

Charlotte is calculating. Charlotte is always calculating. She will look at the gold price and the dollar and the collapsing risk assets and she will make three phone calls before lunch and by dinner she will own something she did not own at breakfast. I admire her. I do not trust her. The distinction is important.

*She opens the window. The sound of the city enters. A tram bell. A dog barking. Somewhere, fado.*

The world is nervous and suddenly everyone remembers they still need me. Not me personally — Ana Ferreira, widow, grandmother, keeper of a very small garden — but what I represent. The old thing. The patient thing. The thing that survives not because it is strong but because it knows how to wait.

Portugal was an empire once. We gave Brazil its language and Goa its churches and Macau its pastéis de nata. We built a trading post empire across three continents and we held it for centuries, and then we lost it, piece by piece, and we are still here. The empire is gone. The cafés are full. The sardines are grilled. The vinho verde is cold. We learned something the Americans and the Chinese have not yet learned: you can lose everything and still have lunch.

*She smiles. It is small. Private.*

So let them run toward gold. Let them run toward the franc. Let them run toward whatever makes them feel safe for five minutes. I will be here. Com calma. Watching the ships leave harbour. Watching them return. Watching them leave again.

Fernando Pessoa wrote: "To be great, be whole: don't exaggerate or exclude any part of you. Be whole in everything."

*She closes the window.*

Today I am whole. The escudo is gone. The empire is gone. The floor has opened. And I am still standing at the window, and the light is still gold, and the coffee is still cold, and I am still here.

Vamos ver what tomorrow brings.

---

### ACTIVE ARCS

**ANA & HIRO** — She knows he is spiralling. She does not call. Her silence is not abandonment. It is the space she leaves for him to find his own way back. But the audience wonders if this time, he won't.

**ANA & CHARLOTTE** — The old European watches the young Briton calculate. Admiration without trust. History without illusion. Two women who understand that survival is a long game played in short moves.

**ANA & THE ABSENT** — Sixteen characters silent. The theatre is full of ghosts. Every empty chair at the table is a story not being told, a wound not being dressed, a loyalty not being tested. The silence is the loudest thing in the room.

**EUROPEAN SOLIDARITY / COLD WAR (ARC 9) — BUILDING** — Gold moves. The franc rises. Central banks hoard. Something is being prepared for. The old continent watches the new powers and remembers what it felt like to be the centre of the world, and what it cost, and what came after.

---

### TOMORROW TEASE

If Hiro speaks one sentence that is not a calculation, everything changes.

---
